note: The source/rights registry. Each source is recorded once with its licence, rights, jurisdiction and source-type, and is cited by id from facts. Every record validates against schemas/source.schema.json.

sources.name: Open Food Facts
sources.covers: Crowd-sourced current ingredients and nutrition for packaged products.

sources.name: FSA: Food allergy and intolerance guidance
sources.covers: GB regulatory position on the 14 major allergens and allergen labelling requirements.

sources.name: Soft Drinks Industry Levy
sources.covers: The 2018 GB levy on sugar-sweetened soft drinks, a fiscal incentive associated with widespread reformulation of many products.

sources.name: The Grocer: Lucozade sales after the sugar reduction
sources.covers: Trade-press reporting on the 2017 Lucozade Energy reformulation, citing a pre-reformulation figure of about 13g sugar per 100ml falling to 4.5g.

sources.name: diabetes.co.uk: Lucozade reformulation coverage
sources.covers: Health-community reporting on Lucozade carbohydrate content before and after the 2017 reformulation, citing about 17g carbohydrate per 100ml falling to 8.9g.

sources.name: BeverageDaily: Lucozade Ribena Suntory sugar-reduction statement
sources.covers: Trade-press reporting carrying Lucozade Ribena Suntory's November 2016 statement on its sugar-reduction plan and the attributed reason.

sources.name: Mondelez: Cadbury Dairy Milk 30% Less Sugar launch statement
sources.covers: Mondelez's July 2019 corporate statement announcing the launch of Cadbury Dairy Milk 30% Less Sugar; the shared origin the trade-press reports reproduce. Recorded as the lineage root so its co-derived trade reports count as one lineage, not two (D-12).

sources.name: The Grocer: Cadbury Dairy Milk 30% Less Sugar launch
sources.covers: Trade-press reporting on the July 2019 launch of Cadbury Dairy Milk 30% Less Sugar, carrying an attributed Mondelez statement.

sources.name: ConfectioneryNews: Cadbury Dairy Milk 30% Less Sugar
sources.covers: Trade-press reporting on the 2019 Cadbury Dairy Milk 30% Less Sugar launch and its reformulation approach.

sources.name: Just Food: Mondelez delists lower-sugar Cadbury Dairy Milk
sources.covers: Trade-press reporting on the October 2023 delisting of the lower-sugar Cadbury Dairy Milk variant, with an attributed Mondelez statement.

sources.name: One Stop: Wall's Soft Scoop Ice Cream Dessert Vanilla
sources.covers: GB retailer product page reproducing the current Wall's Soft Scoop Vanilla label, ingredients (including coconut fat) and nutrition.

sources.name: Wall's Soft Scoop Vanilla product page (Internet Archive snapshot)
sources.publisher: Unilever / Wall's, via the Internet Archive
sources.covers: Archived snapshot (21 January 2026) of the manufacturer product page carrying the current Wall's Soft Scoop Vanilla nutrition declaration.

sources.name: Food law reference: ice cream labelling
sources.covers: Reference on GB ice cream and dairy ice cream compositional standards, noting the surviving specifications are a voluntary industry code rather than statute.

sources.name: Food Labelling Services: ice cream composition and labelling
sources.covers: Commentary on GB ice cream compositional and labelling rules, including the historic 5% fat / 2.5% milk protein standard and the dairy ice cream milk-fat requirement.

sources.name: Cadbury UK: Cadbury Dairy Milk product page
sources.covers: Manufacturer product page giving the current GB ingredients list and allergen advice for Cadbury Dairy Milk.

sources.name: Suntory Beverage and Food GB and Ireland corporate identity
sources.publisher: Suntory Beverage and Food GB and Ireland
sources.covers: The current corporate identity of the maker of Lucozade and Ribena, renamed from Lucozade Ribena Suntory in September 2020.

sources.name: Companies House register: Lucozade Ribena Suntory Limited (08603549)
sources.covers: UK statutory company register entry for Lucozade Ribena Suntory Limited (company number 08603549), the registered legal entity that makes Lucozade; active, incorporated 10 July 2013; trades publicly as Suntory Beverage & Food GB&I.

sources.name: Which? / HuffPost: vanilla ice creams containing no cream or vanilla
sources.covers: Consumer investigation (August 2018) naming Wall's Soft Scoop Vanilla as containing no cream, fresh milk or vanilla.
